test_cases.description: Science podcast — ocean smell / dimethyl sulfide — warm nerdy host, 20 min
test_cases.input_summary: Specific scientific topic, non-technical audience, host voice clearly defined, real research cited, main idea stated
test_cases.output_summary: Full ~2,800-word script with section timestamps, production notes, hook opens on specific sensory scene, transitions written between all segments, closing memorable and specific

test_cases.description: True crime narrative — cold case from the 1970s — 35 min, narrative-first storytelling
test_cases.input_summary: Historical case, character-driven narrative structure, atmospheric opening required, host not visible in the story (pure narrator)
test_cases.output_summary: Script opens with scene-setting in present tense for immediacy; shifts to past tense for historical narrative; production notes mark emotional beats; closing raises unresolved question rather than tidy resolution

test_cases.description: Business/career podcast — 'how to negotiate your first freelance rate' — 15 min, practical educational
test_cases.input_summary: Practical how-to episode, actionable advice, audience is early-career professionals, host voice is direct and encouraging
test_cases.output_summary: Script structured around three principles with clear transitions; host voice is encouraging without being patronizing; closing CTA is specific action (calculate your own rate using a provided method) not just generic subscribe ask

notes: Fluency and coherence are strongest — the scripts read naturally when spoken aloud and maintain clear structure throughout. Actionability is the weakest dimension because the scripts require a real recording and audio production step before they are 'used'; the output is ready for studio but not complete as a deliverable.
